en_definition: relating to measurable amounts or numbers
example1: The study uses quantitative data collected from surveys.
example1_ru: В исследовании используются количественные данные, собранные с помощью опросов.
example2: A quantitative approach relies heavily on statistics.

en_definition: relating to the quality or nature of something rather than numbers
example1: The interviews provided rich qualitative insight into student experiences.
example1_ru: Интервью дали богатую качественную информацию об опыте студентов.
example2: Qualitative research often uses interviews and observations.

en_definition: a single score calculated by combining several individual results
example1: Your composite score combines the quiz, essay, and exam grades.
example1_ru: Ваш совокупный балл объединяет оценки за тест, эссе и экзамен.
example2: A high composite score is needed for the scholarship.

en_definition: increasing steadily as more things are added over time
example1: Your cumulative grade point average includes every semester.
example1_ru: Ваш накопительный средний балл включает все семестры.
example2: The cumulative effect of small errors can be significant.

en_definition: increasing gradually in small steps
example1: The software received incremental updates every month throughout the year.
example1_ru: Программное обеспечение получало постепенные обновления каждый месяц на протяжении года.
example2: Incremental progress is still progress, even if it's slow.

en_definition: growth that increases at an ever faster rate over time
example1: The population showed exponential growth over the past decade.
example1_ru: За последнее десятилетие население демонстрировало экспоненциальный рост.
example2: Exponential growth can quickly overwhelm limited resources.

en_definition: to become or make smaller in size, importance, or strength
example1: The reaction rate diminishes as the reactants are used up.
example1_ru: Скорость реакции уменьшается по мере расходования реагентов.
example2: Public interest in the topic diminished over time.

en_definition: a point where extra effort produces smaller and smaller benefits
example1: After six hours of study, you often reach diminishing returns.
example1_ru: После шести часов учёбы часто наступает закон убывающей отдачи.
example2: Adding more workers led to diminishing returns in productivity.

en_definition: a sudden and large increase in something
example1: There was a surge in applications after the new scholarship was announced.
example1_ru: После объявления новой стипендии произошёл резкий рост числа заявок.
example2: The surge in demand caused a shortage of textbooks.

en_definition: to fall suddenly and steeply in value or amount
example1: Enrollment plunged sharply after the sudden tuition fee increase.
example1_ru: Число зачислений резко упало после внезапного повышения платы за обучение.
example2: Temperatures plunged overnight, and the pipes froze.

en_definition: to increase suddenly and sharply for a short time
example1: Website traffic spiked after the announcement was published.
example1_ru: Трафик на сайте резко подскочил после публикации объявления.
example2: Blood pressure can spike during a stressful exam.

en_definition: to stop increasing and stay at a steady level
example1: Test scores plateaued after the initial period of improvement.
example1_ru: Результаты тестов вышли на плато после первоначального периода улучшения.
example2: Sales plateaued once the market became saturated.

en_definition: to gradually decrease until it stops or almost stops
example1: Interest in the topic tapered off after the initial excitement.
example1_ru: Интерес к теме постепенно снизился после первоначального ажиотажа.
example2: The rainfall tapered off by the afternoon.

en_definition: to stop rising or falling and become steady
example1: Prices leveled off after several months of increases.
example1_ru: Цены стабилизировались после нескольких месяцев роста.
example2: The graph shows the temperature leveling off near zero.

en_definition: the ability to critically evaluate information from various media sources
example1: The course teaches media literacy to help spot false information.
example1_ru: Курс обучает медиаграмотности, чтобы помочь распознавать ложную информацию.
example2: Media literacy is increasingly important in the digital age.

en_definition: false or inaccurate information spread without intent to deceive
example1: Misinformation about the vaccine spread quickly across several social media platforms.
example1_ru: Дезинформация о вакцине быстро распространилась в нескольких социальных сетях.
example2: Teachers encourage students to fact-check before sharing misinformation.

en_definition: the quality of being trusted and believed
example1: Check the credibility of a source before citing it in your essay.
example1_ru: Проверяйте достоверность источника, прежде чем цитировать его в эссе.
example2: The scandal damaged the credibility of the entire report.

en_definition: the quality of being based on facts rather than personal feelings
example1: Journalists strive for objectivity when reporting the news.
example1_ru: Журналисты стремятся к объективности при подаче новостей.
example2: The report was criticized for lacking objectivity.

en_definition: a story important enough to be printed on the first page of a newspaper
example1: The university's discovery became front-page news across the entire country.
example1_ru: Открытие университета стало новостью на первой полосе по всей стране.
example2: The scandal was front-page news for over a week.

en_definition: a newspaper with small pages, often featuring sensational stories
example1: The tabloid printed an exaggerated version of the story.
example1_ru: Таблоид напечатал преувеличенную версию этой истории.
example2: Academic sources are usually more reliable than tabloids.

en_definition: to spread or pass around, especially information
example1: The rumor circulated quickly around the campus before anyone confirmed it.
example1_ru: Слух быстро распространился по кампусу, прежде чем кто-либо его подтвердил.
example2: Please circulate the meeting agenda to all members.

en_definition: spreading very quickly online among many people
example1: The video of the experiment went viral within a day.
example1_ru: Видео с экспериментом стало вирусным за один день.
example2: A viral post can reach millions of people quickly.

en_definition: a person who can affect the opinions of others, especially through social media
example1: The influencer promoted the new app to millions of followers.
example1_ru: Инфлюенсер продвигал новое приложение среди миллионов подписчиков.
example2: Companies often collaborate with influencers for marketing campaigns.

en_definition: an arrangement to pay regularly to receive a service or publication
example1: The library offers a free subscription to several academic journals.
example1_ru: Библиотека предлагает бесплатную подписку на несколько научных журналов.
example2: Cancel your subscription before the trial period ends.

en_definition: to send out a program on television, radio, or online as it happens
example1: The graduation ceremony was broadcast live on the university website.
example1_ru: Церемония вручения дипломов транслировалась в прямом эфире на сайте университета.
example2: The lecture was broadcast live to students studying remotely.

ru_translation: пригородная зона (жители которой ездят на работу в город)
en_definition: the residential area outside a city where people who work in the city live
example1: Housing in the commuter belt is usually cheaper than in the city.
example1_ru: Жильё в пригородной зоне обычно дешевле, чем в городе.
example2: Many students live in the commuter belt and travel to campus daily.

en_definition: the uncontrolled spread of a city into surrounding areas
example1: Urban sprawl has reduced the amount of farmland near the city.
example1_ru: Разрастание городов сократило площадь сельскохозяйственных земель вблизи города.
example2: Better planning could help control urban sprawl.

en_definition: the basic systems and structures a country or city needs to function
example1: The city invested heavily in transport infrastructure last year.
example1_ru: В прошлом году город вложил значительные средства в транспортную инфраструктуру.
example2: Poor infrastructure makes the daily commute difficult.

en_definition: a state of being overcrowded, especially referring to traffic
example1: Traffic congestion is worst during rush hour near the campus.
example1_ru: Дорожные заторы наиболее серьёзны в час пик возле кампуса.
example2: The new bus lane was built to reduce congestion.

en_definition: at a time when demand or use is lower than usual
example1: Traveling off-peak is usually cheaper and less crowded.
example1_ru: Поездки во внепиковое время обычно дешевле и менее многолюдны.
example2: The library is quieter during off-peak hours.

en_definition: buses, trains, and other transport available to the general public
example1: Students receive a discount on public transit passes.
example1_ru: Студенты получают скидку на проездные в общественном транспорте.
example2: Public transit is often cheaper than driving to campus.

en_definition: an arrangement where several people share a car journey
example1: They organized a carpool to save money on gas.
example1_ru: Они организовали совместную поездку на машине, чтобы сэкономить на бензине.
example2: Joining a carpool reduces traffic and pollution.

en_definition: a service that connects people to share vehicle journeys, often via an app
example1: Many students use ride-sharing apps when public transport is unavailable.
example1_ru: Многие студенты пользуются приложениями для совместных поездок, когда общественный транспорт недоступен.
example2: Ride-sharing can be cheaper than taking a taxi alone.

en_definition: an area where cars are not allowed and only walking is permitted
example1: The city center became a pedestrian zone last year.
example1_ru: В прошлом году центр города стал пешеходной зоной.
example2: Cafes and shops line the busy pedestrian zone.

en_definition: a part of the road reserved for bicycles
example1: New bike lanes were added around the university campus.
example1_ru: Вокруг университетского кампуса появились новые велосипедные дорожки.
example2: Always ride in the bike lane where available.

en_definition: a circular junction where traffic flows in one direction around a central island
example1: Turn right at the roundabout to reach the campus.
example1_ru: Поверните направо на кольцевой развязке, чтобы добраться до кампуса.
example2: The roundabout helps traffic flow more smoothly than a crossroads.

en_definition: a passage running under a road or railway for pedestrians or vehicles
example1: Use the underpass to cross safely under the busy road.
example1_ru: Используйте подземный переход, чтобы безопасно пересечь оживлённую дорогу.
example2: The underpass connects the two halves of the campus.

en_definition: a fee charged for using a particular road or bridge
example1: Drivers must pay a toll to use the highway into the city.
example1_ru: Водители должны платить дорожную пошлину за проезд по шоссе в город.
example2: The toll increases during peak travel hours.

en_definition: a route used temporarily instead of the usual one, often due to roadworks
example1: Follow the signs for the diversion around the closed street.
example1_ru: Следуйте указателям объезда закрытой улицы.
example2: The diversion added twenty minutes to the journey.

en_definition: arriving or doing something at the expected time
example1: Punctual students always arrive before the lecture begins.
example1_ru: Пунктуальные студенты всегда приходят до начала лекции.
example2: Being punctual shows respect for other people's time.

en_definition: at exactly the stated time
example1: The lecture starts at nine o'clock on the dot.
example1_ru: Лекция начинается ровно в девять часов.
example2: She arrived on the dot for her appointment.

en_definition: the predicted time when a person or vehicle will arrive
example1: The app shows the estimated time of arrival for the next bus.
example1_ru: В приложении показано расчётное время прибытия следующего автобуса.
example2: Please share your estimated time of arrival with the driver.

en_definition: to be as good as expected or required
example1: The final report did not measure up to the professor's expectations.
example1_ru: Итоговый отчёт не соответствовал ожиданиям профессора.
example2: His performance measured up well against the other candidates.

en_definition: to estimate or measure a quality or amount
example1: The survey helps gauge student satisfaction with the course.
example1_ru: Опрос помогает оценить удовлетворённость студентов курсом.
example2: It's hard to gauge how difficult the exam will be.

en_definition: to adjust an instrument again to restore its accuracy
example1: We had to recalibrate the sensor after it gave inconsistent readings.
example1_ru: Нам пришлось перекалибровать датчик после того, как он дал противоречивые показания.
example2: Recalibrate the scale every time you move it to a new location.

en_definition: the acceptable range of variation from an exact measurement
example1: The part must be manufactured within a very small tolerance.
example1_ru: Деталь должна изготавливаться в пределах очень малого допуска.
example2: Any measurement outside the tolerance is considered a defect.

en_definition: a small regular increase in a value
example1: The temperature was recorded in increments of one degree.
example1_ru: Температура фиксировалась с шагом в один градус.
example2: Salaries increase in small increments each year.

en_definition: the ratio used to convert one unit or currency to another
example1: Check the conversion rate before changing your money.
example1_ru: Проверьте курс обмена перед тем, как менять деньги.
example2: The conversion rate between miles and kilometers is roughly 1.6.

en_definition: a system of measurement based on units of ten, such as meters and kilograms
example1: Most countries use the metric system for everyday measurement.
example1_ru: Большинство стран используют метрическую систему для повседневных измерений.
example2: Scientific work almost always uses the metric system.

en_definition: the digits in a number that carry meaning for its precision
example1: Round your final answer to three significant figures.
example1_ru: Округлите итоговый ответ до трёх значащих цифр.
example2: Extra significant figures can give a false sense of precision.

en_definition: the position of a digit after the decimal point in a number
example1: Give your answer to two decimal places for full marks.
example1_ru: Дайте ответ с точностью до двух десятичных знаков, чтобы получить полный балл.
example2: Rounding to one decimal place is sufficient here.

en_definition: the process of changing a value from one unit of measurement to another
example1: Unit conversion is a common source of errors in physics homework.
example1_ru: Перевод единиц измерения — частый источник ошибок в домашних заданиях по физике.
example2: Double-check your unit conversion before submitting the answer.

en_definition: a system of symbols used to represent numbers, ideas, or information
example1: Scientific notation makes very large numbers easier to write.
example1_ru: Научная запись делает очень большие числа проще для записи.
example2: Use standard notation when writing chemical formulas.

en_definition: the number below the line in a fraction
example1: The denominator cannot be equal to zero in any fraction.
example1_ru: Знаменатель не может быть равен нулю ни в одной дроби.
example2: Find a common denominator before adding the fractions.

en_definition: the number above the line in a fraction
example1: The numerator shows how many parts you have.
example1_ru: Числитель показывает, сколько частей у вас есть.
example2: Multiply both the numerator and denominator by the same number.

en_definition: the value obtained by dividing one by a given number
example1: The reciprocal of four is one quarter of the original value.
example1_ru: Обратная величина четырёх — одна четвёртая от исходного значения.
example2: Multiply by the reciprocal instead of dividing by the fraction.

en_definition: a whole number that is not a fraction
example1: The answer must be expressed as a positive integer.
example1_ru: Ответ должен быть выражен положительным целым числом.
example2: Negative integers are still whole numbers.

en_definition: a number greater than one that can only be divided by itself and one
example1: Seven is a prime number because it has no other factors.
example1_ru: Семь — простое число, потому что у него нет других делителей.
example2: The algorithm checks whether a large number is prime.

en_definition: to break a number or expression into its multiplying parts
example1: Factor the equation before solving for x in the next step.
example1_ru: Разложите уравнение на множители, прежде чем решать его относительно x на следующем шаге.
example2: Twelve factors into two, two, and three.

en_definition: a small number showing how many times a value is multiplied by itself
example1: In this expression, the exponent tells you to square the base.
example1_ru: В этом выражении показатель степени говорит о возведении основания в квадрат.
example2: A negative exponent represents a reciprocal value.

en_definition: the value that, multiplied by itself three times, gives a given number
example1: The cube root of twenty-seven is three, since three cubed is twenty-seven.
example1_ru: Кубический корень из двадцати семи равен трём, так как три в кубе равно двадцати семи.
example2: Use a calculator to find the cube root of large numbers.

en_definition: a flat shape with three or more straight sides
example1: A hexagon is a polygon with six sides.
example1_ru: Шестиугольник — это многоугольник с шестью сторонами.
example2: Calculate the area of the polygon using the given formula.

en_definition: the distance around the edge of a circle
example1: The circumference of the circle is given by two pi r.
example1_ru: Длина окружности круга вычисляется по формуле два пи эр.
example2: Measure the circumference of the cylinder with a tape measure.

en_definition: the total area of all the outer surfaces of a solid object
example1: Calculate the surface area of the cube before painting it.
example1_ru: Вычислите площадь поверхности куба перед его покраской.
example2: A larger surface area allows for faster heat loss.

en_definition: the shape seen when an object is cut straight across
example1: The diagram shows a cross-section of the human heart.
example1_ru: На диаграмме показано поперечное сечение сердца человека.
example2: The cross-section of the pipe reveals its inner diameter.

en_definition: a flat surface used for plotting points with x and y coordinates
example1: Plot the points on the coordinate plane to see the pattern.
example1_ru: Отметьте точки на координатной плоскости, чтобы увидеть закономерность.
example2: Each point on the coordinate plane has an x and a y value.

en_definition: a quantity with both size and direction
example1: Velocity is a vector because it has both speed and direction.
example1_ru: Скорость — это вектор, так как у неё есть и величина, и направление.
example2: Draw an arrow to represent the vector on the diagram.

en_definition: a quantity that has only size and no direction
example1: Mass is a scalar quantity, unlike force, which has direction.
example1_ru: Масса — скалярная величина, в отличие от силы, у которой есть направление.
example2: Temperature is measured as a scalar value.

en_definition: a set of numbers or events arranged in a particular order
example1: Identify the pattern in the following number sequence.
example1_ru: Определите закономерность в следующей числовой последовательности.
example2: The steps must be completed in the correct sequence.

en_definition: a lack of balance or equality between two sides of something
example1: The asymmetry of the molecule affects its chemical properties.
example1_ru: Асимметрия молекулы влияет на её химические свойства.
example2: There is a clear asymmetry in the distribution of income.

en_definition: a basic belief that is taken for granted without being stated
example1: The model's underlying assumption is that all consumers act rationally.
example1_ru: Основное допущение модели заключается в том, что все потребители действуют рационально.
example2: Question the underlying assumption before accepting the conclusion.

en_definition: a mistaken belief based on unsound reasoning
example1: The essay identifies a common fallacy in the argument.
example1_ru: В эссе выявляется распространённая логическая ошибка в аргументе.
example2: It is a fallacy to assume correlation always means causation.

en_definition: a distortion in results caused by a non-random sample
example1: Selection bias occurred because only volunteers took part in the study.
example1_ru: Систематическая ошибка отбора возникла, потому что в исследовании участвовали только добровольцы.
example2: Random sampling helps to reduce selection bias.

en_definition: the tendency to favor information that confirms existing beliefs
example1: Confirmation bias can lead researchers to ignore contrary evidence.
example1_ru: Предвзятость подтверждения может привести исследователей к игнорированию противоречащих доказательств.
example2: Be aware of confirmation bias when reviewing your own data.

en_definition: to select only the evidence that supports your point while ignoring the rest
example1: It is misleading to cherry-pick data that supports only one side.
example1_ru: Вводить в заблуждение — выборочно использовать данные, подтверждающие только одну сторону.
example2: The report was criticized for cherry-picking favorable statistics.

en_definition: a weakened version of an opponent's argument that is easier to attack
example1: His response was a straw man that misrepresented the original claim.
example1_ru: Его ответ был подменой тезиса, искажающей исходное утверждение.
example2: Avoid building a straw man argument in your essay.

en_definition: an argument that one small step will lead to a chain of negative events
example1: The argument relies on a slippery slope without real evidence.
example1_ru: Аргумент опирается на «скользкий путь» без реальных доказательств.
example2: Critics called the proposal a slippery slope toward stricter rules.

en_definition: an argument in which the conclusion is used as its own evidence
example1: The proof contains circular reasoning and needs to be reworked.
example1_ru: Доказательство содержит порочный круг в рассуждении, и его нужно переработать.
example2: Avoid circular reasoning by finding independent evidence.

ru_translation: неподтверждённые данные, основанные на отдельных случаях
en_definition: evidence based on personal stories rather than systematic research
example1: Anecdotal evidence alone is not enough to prove a scientific claim.
example1_ru: Одних неподтверждённых данных недостаточно, чтобы доказать научное утверждение.
example2: The report relies too heavily on anecdotal evidence.

en_definition: evidence gathered through observation or experiment
example1: The theory needs empirical evidence before it can be accepted.
example1_ru: Теории требуются эмпирические доказательства, прежде чем её можно будет принять.
example2: Empirical evidence supports the claim made in the introduction.

en_definition: checked and approved by other experts before publication
example1: Use peer-reviewed journal articles for your literature review.
example1_ru: Используйте статьи из рецензируемых журналов для вашего обзора литературы.
example2: Peer-reviewed research is generally more reliable than blog posts.

en_definition: an original document or first-hand account used as evidence
example1: Historians rely on primary sources such as letters and diaries.
example1_ru: Историки опираются на первоисточники, такие как письма и дневники.
example2: Always try to cite the primary source rather than a summary.

en_definition: a source that interprets or analyzes primary sources
example1: Textbooks are usually considered secondary sources rather than primary sources.
example1_ru: Учебники обычно считаются вторичными источниками, а не первоисточниками.
example2: Compare the secondary source with the original data if possible.

en_definition: able to be checked or proven to be true
example1: Make sure every claim in your essay is verifiable.
example1_ru: Убедитесь, что каждое утверждение в вашем эссе проверяемо.
example2: The data must be verifiable through an independent source.

en_definition: not based on facts or evidence
example1: The rumor turned out to be completely unfounded.
example1_ru: Слух оказался совершенно необоснованным.
example2: Reviewers dismissed the claim as unfounded speculation.

en_definition: to form an opinion without firm evidence
example1: Scientists speculate that the effect is caused by temperature changes.
example1_ru: Учёные предполагают, что эффект вызван изменениями температуры.
example2: It is unwise to speculate before all the data is collected.

en_definition: an opinion formed without complete proof
example1: The mathematician's conjecture remained unproven for decades before finally being solved.
example1_ru: Гипотеза математика оставалась недоказанной на протяжении десятилетий, прежде чем была наконец решена.
example2: This is only conjecture until we gather more data.

en_definition: to show that a widely held belief is false
example1: The documentary aims to debunk common myths about nutrition.
example1_ru: Документальный фильм направлен на развенчание распространённых мифов о питании.
example2: Scientists debunked the claim with a controlled experiment.

en_definition: to admit that something is true, especially after first disagreeing
example1: She conceded that the opposing argument had some merit.
example1_ru: Она признала, что в противоположном аргументе есть доля истины.
example2: He conceded the point but still disagreed with the conclusion.

en_definition: to make different things consistent or compatible with each other
example1: The committee worked to harmonize the two grading systems.
example1_ru: Комитет работал над тем, чтобы согласовать две системы оценивания.
example2: The new policy harmonizes rules across all departments.

en_definition: showing subtle differences in meaning or opinion
example1: The essay presents a nuanced view of the debate.
example1_ru: В эссе представлен нюансированный взгляд на дискуссию.
example2: Her nuanced argument avoided simple black-and-white claims.

en_definition: causing strong disagreement between opposing groups
example1: The proposal proved to be a polarizing topic among staff.
example1_ru: Предложение оказалось темой, вызывающей резкое расхождение мнений среди сотрудников.
example2: Social media often amplifies polarizing opinions.

en_definition: shared beliefs or interests that different people or groups can agree on
example1: The two sides finally found common ground on funding priorities.
example1_ru: Обе стороны наконец нашли точки соприкосновения по приоритетам финансирования.
example2: Look for common ground before starting the negotiation.

en_definition: a view that differs from the majority opinion
example1: One committee member submitted a dissenting opinion in writing.
example1_ru: Один из членов комитета письменно изложил особое мнение.
example2: The report included a dissenting opinion from the minority group.

en_definition: a position that satisfies both sides of a disagreement to some extent
example1: The two departments finally found a middle ground on the budget.
example1_ru: Два отдела наконец нашли компромисс по бюджету.
example2: A good negotiator looks for middle ground between opposing sides.

en_definition: a balance achieved by giving up one thing in return for another
example1: There is always a trade-off between speed and accuracy.
example1_ru: Всегда существует компромисс между скоростью и точностью.
example2: The trade-off of studying abroad is being far from family.

en_definition: the negative aspect of a situation
example1: The main downside of the plan is its high cost.
example1_ru: Главный недостаток плана — его высокая стоимость.
example2: Every option has an upside and a downside.

en_definition: a positive or advantageous quality of an argument or plan
example1: One strength of the proposal is its low implementation cost.
example1_ru: Одна из сильных сторон предложения — низкая стоимость внедрения.
example2: Discuss the strengths and weaknesses of both theories.

en_definition: a fault or weakness in a person, plan, or system
example1: The reviewer pointed out several shortcomings in the study's design.
example1_ru: Рецензент указал на несколько недостатков в дизайне исследования.
example2: Despite its shortcomings, the plan was approved.

en_definition: so interesting or convincing that it holds your full attention
example1: She made a compelling case for changing the policy.
example1_ru: Она привела убедительные доводы в пользу изменения политики.
example2: The documentary presents a compelling argument about climate change.
